document_id: 1.DIRECTIVE (UE) 20192161 DU PARLEMENT EUROPÉEN ET DU CONSEIL.html
category:
Category: TRADE

Explanation: This appears to be a EU directive focused on consumer protection rules and trade practices, including sanctions for violations of consumer rights and regulations for businesses operating in the EU market. The document discusses modifications to existing consumer protection directives and enforcement measures.

extracted_info.title: DIRECTIVE (EU) 2019/2161 on better enforcement and modernization of Union consumer protection rules
extracted_info.scope: Consumer protection rules and trade practices in the EU internal market
key_requirements: Rules on consumer reviews and endorsements
Consumer rights for digital content and services

key_requirement_details.requirement: Price reduction announcement must show prior price

metrics.description: Minimum period for prior price reference

key_requirement_details.compliance_action: Display previous lowest price from last 30 days before reduction

key_requirement_details.requirement: Online marketplace transparency on ranking parameters
key_requirement_details.compliance_action: Disclose main parameters determining ranking of offers

reporting_requirements.description: Member States must notify Commission of national provisions on penalties
reporting_requirements.frequency: Once initially and upon any changes

monetary_thresholds.description: Maximum fine when annual turnover information unavailable

quantitative_limits.description: Maximum fine as proportion of trader's annual turnover

enforcement_agencies: Administrative bodies designated by Member States

phased_milestones: Member States to adopt measures by 2021-11-28

notes: Member States may impose stricter consumer protection measures in certain areas
Directive modifies several existing EU consumer protection directives
Special provisions for digital content and services
